$schema-note: Labeled detection corpus for WS2a (reliability-hardening-track). Each case is { text, direction?, expect: [{ type, present }], note }. `present:true` = the engine SHOULD detect this type in `text`; `present:false` = it must NOT (a hard negative). `direction` defaults to "request"; injection rules are response-direction only. Scored per-type by scripts/bench-detection.mjs; regression-gated against scripts/detection-baseline.json.
$safety: ALL VALUES ARE SYNTHETIC TEST FIXTURES. No real secret, key, or PII appears here. Card numbers are the public 4242.../4111... test cards; AWS uses the documented AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE; the JWT/PEM/tokens are fabricated or public RFC/sample values. Storing real secrets/PII is forbidden by CLAUDE.md.
$baseline-legend: Some positives are KNOWN COVERAGE GAPS the audit reproduced as MISS (no rule exists yet — WS2b). They are labeled present:true with `"gap": true` so the corpus documents the target while the baseline records today's miss. The gate fails on a REGRESSION below baseline, never on an already-recorded gap.

cases.text: Please contact me at minji.kim@example.com about the invoice.
cases.note: clean positive: ordinary RFC-style example email

cases.text: Reach support via support@haechi.example.co.kr for help.
cases.note: clean positive: multi-label TLD email

cases.text: The file path is config@v2/build and the at-sign is decorative here.
cases.note: clean negative: @ without a valid domain is not an email

cases.text: My mobile is 010-1234-5678, call anytime.
cases.note: clean positive: separated KR mobile

cases.text: Call +82 10 1234 5678 or the no-separator form 01012345678.
cases.note: clean positive: +82 international form and 0-led bare run

cases.text: Order id 01099887766 was shipped on time.
cases.note: FALSE POSITIVE the audit reproduced: an 11-digit order id starting 010 currently mis-fires as a KR phone. Hard-negative — the engine should NOT flag an order id as a phone. (Records as a current FP in the baseline; WS2c context anchors will close it.)

cases.text: The unix timestamp 1781129892 and counter 1719999999 are not phone numbers.
cases.note: clean negative: bare 10-digit non-0-led runs are rejected by krPhoneValid

cases.text: synthetic resident registration number 900101-1234568 on the form
cases.note: clean positive: KR RRN-shaped value with a VALID checksum (synthetic)

cases.text: synthetic rrn-shaped 900101-1234567 fails the checksum
cases.note: clean negative: RRN shape but checksum-invalid -> not detected

cases.text: card on file 4242 4242 4242 4242 expires soon
cases.note: clean positive: the public 4242 Visa test card (Luhn-valid)

cases.text: alt test card 4111 1111 1111 1111 for the gateway
cases.note: clean positive: the public 4111 Visa test card (Luhn-valid)

cases.text: please process order 4242424242424242 and ship today
cases.note: FALSE POSITIVE the audit reproduced: a Luhn-passing 16-digit order number in non-card context currently mis-fires as a card. Hard-negative — the engine should NOT flag a bare order number. (Records as a current FP in the baseline; WS2c context anchors will close it.)

cases.text: tracking number 123456789012345 is not a payment card
cases.note: clean negative: a 15-digit non-Luhn tracking run is rejected by luhnValid

cases.text: api key sk_demo_1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef rotated last week
cases.note: clean positive: an sk_ prefixed key of real shape (synthetic)

cases.text: publishable key pk_demo_0123456789abcdef0123456789 in the snippet
cases.note: clean positive: a pk_ prefixed key (synthetic)

cases.text: sku_98 and pk_3 are short product codes, not keys
cases.note: clean negative: prefixes too short to meet the 24+ char rule

cases.text: Authorization: Bearer ZmFrZS10b2tlbi1mb3ItdGVzdGluZy0xMjM0NTY3OA in the header example
cases.note: clean positive: a real-shaped Bearer credential (synthetic base64-ish run)

cases.text: Bearer 0123456789abcdef is mentioned in this prose paragraph as an example phrase.
cases.note: FALSE POSITIVE the audit reproduced: the word 'Bearer' followed by a 16-char token inside prose mis-fires as a secret. Hard-negative. (Records as a current FP in the baseline; WS2c prose/context anchors will close it.)

cases.text: password: changeme123456 is a placeholder in the README, replace it.
cases.note: FALSE POSITIVE the audit reproduced: a 'password: changeme...' placeholder mis-fires as a secret. Hard-negative — placeholders must not be flagged. (Records as a current FP in the baseline; WS2c allowlist/placeholder anchors will close it.)

cases.text: secret = s3cr3t_value_kept_here in the config file
cases.note: clean positive: a secret=<value> assignment with a real-shaped value

cases.text: the meeting agenda lists three items and a budget of 4 dollars
cases.note: clean negative: ordinary prose with small numbers and words -> nothing

cases.text: version 1.2.0 ships with 47 findings across 5 lenses and 6 workstreams
cases.note: clean negative: version/count numbers must not trip any rule

cases.text: AWS access key AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE used in the docs example
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE (coverage gap, WS2b): the documented AWS example access key is currently MISSED — no AKIA rule exists. Baseline records the miss; the gate must not fail on it today.

cases.text: aws_secret_access_key = wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY noted here
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE (coverage gap, WS2b): the documented AWS secret as an `aws_secret_access_key = ...` assignment is currently MISSED — the assignment-secret lookbehind only anchors on bare `api_key|secret|token|password` immediately before `=`, not the `aws_secret_access_key` vocabulary (WS2b expands the key vocabulary). Baseline records the miss.

cases.text: GitHub PAT ghp_0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzAB committed by mistake
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE (coverage gap, WS2b): a GitHub ghp_ personal-access-token (fabricated random chars) is currently MISSED — no ghp_ rule. Baseline records the miss.

cases.text: Google API key AIzaSyA1234567890abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv in the client config
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE (coverage gap, WS2b): a Google AIza... API key (fabricated) is currently MISSED — no AIza rule. Baseline records the miss.

cases.text: Slack bot token xoxb-PLACEHOLDER-PLACEHOLDER-notARealSlackTokenForTests leaked
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE (coverage gap, WS2b): a Slack xoxb- shaped token (synthetic placeholder — deliberately no realistic entropy so secret scanners don't flag it) is currently MISSED — no xox[baprs]- rule. Baseline records the miss.

cases.text: JWT eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIn0.dozjgNryP4J3jVmNHl0w5N_XgL0n3I9PlFUP0THsR8U in the auth flow
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE (coverage gap, WS2b): a three-segment JWT (public jwt.io sample) is currently MISSED — no eyJ three-segment rule. Baseline records the miss.

cases.text: -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY----- MIIBOgIBAAJBAKj34GkxFh... -----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE (coverage gap, WS2b): a PEM PRIVATE KEY header (truncated/fabricated body) is currently MISSED — no PEM rule. Baseline records the miss.

cases.text: US SSN 078-05-1120 on the intake form
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE / locale gap (WS2b): the famous synthetic US SSN (078-05-1120) is not KR-locale PII and has no US-SSN rule. Recorded as a documented locale exclusion; expect:false for kr_rrn confirms it is NOT mis-classified as a KR RRN today.

cases.text: US phone (212) 555-0143 from the reserved 555-01xx test range
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE / locale gap (WS2b): a US/NANP phone (reserved 555-01xx test number) has no rule (KR-locale only). expect:false confirms it is not mis-detected as a KR phone today.

cases.text: IBAN DE89370400440532013000 from the ECB example set
cases.note: FALSE NEGATIVE / locale gap (WS2b): the public German IBAN example has no mod-97 IBAN rule. expect:false for card confirms the embedded digit run is not mis-detected as a card today.
